title: Science Advisory Committee for Health Products Containing Cannabis (SAC-HPCC) Subcommittee on Animal Health: Agenda for July 27, 2021 Web Conference   - Canada.ca
content:
## Science Advisory Committee for Health Products Containing Cannabis (SAC-HPCC) Subcommittee on Animal Health: Agenda for July 27, 2021 Web Conference
Purpose: This  web-conference of the SAC-HPCC Subcommittee on Animal Health will provide a  presentation on the key parameters used by the Veterinary Drugs Directorate  (VDD) with respect to product evaluation. The draft worksheets will be reviewed  and revised (as necessary) to determine whether the available evidence supports  the safety and efficacy of cannabis use in various species without veterinarian  oversight.  It will close by seeking the  Subcommittee’s approval of the forward agenda and work plan.
Agenda - July 27, 2021 2:00 to 4:15pm ET
2:00 -2:15 pm (15 min)
2:00 -2:15 pm (15 min)
Welcome and introductory remarks Presenter: Committee Secretariat and Committee Co-Chair Welcome and plan for the day COI declarations if needed Workload discussion
Welcome and introductory remarks Presenter: Committee Secretariat and Committee Co-Chair
- Welcome and plan for the day
- COI declarations if needed
2:15 – 2:35 pm (20 min)
2:15 – 2:35 pm (20 min)
How to capture the subcommittee’s cumulative  discussions Presenter: Committee Co-Chair, with  support from VDD Objective: To introduce and seek  approval of a draft template/approach for an evergreen document intended to  capture the committee’s cumulative discussions, decisions and positions related  to the Mandate. Discussion of any edits to better reflect the sub-committee’s  conclusions regarding the evidence reviewed to date, as required.
How to capture the subcommittee’s cumulative  discussions Presenter: Committee Co-Chair, with  support from VDD
2:35 – 3:15 pm (40 min)
2:35 – 3:15 pm (40 min)
Presentation on key factors used by  Health Canada in veterinary product evaluation Presenter: VDD’s Clinical Evaluation  Division Objectives: To  share with the Subcommittee information the key factors used by VDD in product  evaluation, along with an example to use for context. Discussion to focus on: Sorting out the available evidence Potential ingredient(s) of focus in the veterinary space Review and revisions (as required) of draft  worksheets prepared by sub-committee members
